She will definitely give birth to a boy. (Zhou Riyong said: If it is known to be a girl, then the method can be applied. Or if it was first a boy, what should be done? I have heard that there is a method to determine the gender by calculating the mother's age and the date of conception. If the result is odd, it will be a boy; if even, a girl. If it is known to be a girl, then the method can be applied.)

    A pregnant woman should not be allowed to see ugly or evil things, strange birds and beasts, or unusual flavors in food. She should not be allowed to see bears, tigers, and leopards, nor should she see shooting birds and pheasants. She should not eat the heart of a cow, the meat of a white dog, or the head of a carp. She should not sit on an unaligned mat or eat food that is not properly cut. She should listen to the recitation of poetry and books, and not listen to lewd sounds or look at improper sights. By doing so, the child will definitely be virtuous, upright, and long-lived. This is called the method of prenatal education by parents. (Lu said: If the fetus receives pure and clear nourishment, it will be born as a benevolent and wise person. If it receives chaotic energy, it will be born as a greedy and licentious person. The child is influenced by the mother's energy.)

    Therefore, in ancient times, a pregnant woman must be careful about what she is influenced by. If influenced by good, she will be good; if influenced by evil, she will be evil. A pregnant woman should not eat rabbit meat, nor should she see rabbits, as it will cause the child's lips to be split. She should not eat ginger, as it will cause the child to have extra fingers. [50]

    异説云瞽瞍夫妇凶顽而生舜叔梁纥淫夫也征在失行也加又野合而生仲尼焉安在有胎教也【卢氏曰夫甲及寅年生者圣以年在嵗德在甲寅之年生者则然矣亦由先天也亦由父母气也古者元气清故多圣今者俗淫隂浊故无圣人也】
    　　豫章郡衣冠人有数妇暴面于道寻道争分铢以给其夫舆马衣资及举孝亷更取富者一切皆给先者虽有数年之勤妇子满堂室犹放黜以避后人
    　　诸逺方山郡幽僻处出蜜蜡人徃徃以桶聚蜂每年一取
    　　逺方诸山蜜蜡处以木为器中开小孔以蜜蜡涂器内外令遍春月蜂将生育时捕取三两头着器中蜂飞去寻将伴来经日渐益遂持器归
    　　人借带眠者则梦蛇 [51]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [51]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 异説云 - Formal way to introduce a different or alternative viewpoint or statement.
    • 瞽瞍夫妇凶顽 - Gushu and his wife were wicked and stubborn, where '瞽瞍' (Gushu) is a name for a historical figure known for his wickedness, and '凶顽' (wicked and stubborn) describes his character.
    • 野合而生仲尼 - Born from a union in the wild, referring to Confucius' birth under unusual circumstances.
    • 安在有胎教也 - How can there be prenatal education? This is a rhetorical question challenging the notion of prenatal education.
    • 豫章郡衣冠人 - People of high social standing in Yuzhang Prefecture, where '衣冠人' (people of high social standing) refers to the elite class.
    • 暴面于道 - Exposed their faces on the road, suggesting public exposure or humiliation.
    • 寻道争分铢以给其夫 - Struggled to give even the smallest amount to their husbands, indicating extreme poverty and hardship.
    • 舆马衣资 - Carriages, horses, clothes, and provisions, referring to material wealth.
    • 举孝亷 - Elected for filial piety and integrity, a title given to those who were recognized for their moral virtues.
    • 更取富者 - Instead, they chose the rich, indicating a preference for wealth over virtue.
    • 先者虽有数年之勤 - Those who came first, despite years of hard work, referring to the initial group of people who had worked diligently.
    • 妇子满堂室 - Wives and children filled the house, indicating a large family.
    • 放黜以避后人 - Were dismissed and replaced by later people, suggesting a shift in favor towards newer individuals.
    • 远方山郡幽僻处 - Remote and secluded areas in distant mountainous regions, describing isolated places.
    • 以木为器 - Made wooden containers, referring to simple tools or vessels.
    • 以蜜蜡涂器内外令遍 - Coated the inside and outside of the containers with honey and wax, indicating a method of attracting bees.
    • 春月蜂将生育时 - During the spring when bees are about to reproduce, indicating a specific time of year.
    • 捕取三两头着器中 - Captured a few and placed them in the containers, describing the process of capturing bees.
    • 经日渐益 - Gradually increased over time, indicating a progressive accumulation.
    • 遂持器归 - Finally carried the containers back home, indicating the completion of the process.
    • 人借带眠者则梦蛇 - Those who sleep while wearing a belt dream of snakes, a superstitious belief or folk tale.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 瞽瞍夫妇 - Refers to Gushu and his wife, historical figures known for their wickedness.
    • 仲尼 - Refers to Confucius, a renowned philosopher and founder of Confucianism.
    • 豫章郡 - Refers to Yuzhang Prefecture, a historical region in modern-day Jiangxi Province.
    • 衣冠人 - Refers to the elite class, often associated with high social status and moral virtue.
    • 孝亷 - Refers to a title given to those recognized for their filial piety and integrity, a value highly esteemed in Chinese culture.
    • 远方山郡 - Refers to remote and secluded areas, often associated with simplicity and isolation.
    • 蜜蜡 - Refers to honey and wax, commonly used in beekeeping and traditional crafts.

    Proper Nouns

    • 瞽瞍 - A historical figure known for his wickedness.
    • 仲尼 - Confucius, a renowned philosopher and founder of Confucianism.
    • 豫章郡 - Yuzhang Prefecture, a historical region in modern-day Jiangxi Province.
    • 孝亷 - A title given to those recognized for their filial piety and integrity.
    • 远方山郡 - Remote and secluded areas in distant mountainous regions.

    A different account states that Gushu and his wicked wife bore Shun, while Shuliang He, a licentious man, and Zheng Zai, who had lost her moral bearings, gave birth to Confucius through an unconventional union. How can there be prenatal education in such circumstances? [Lu Shi said: Those born in the year of Jia or Yin are considered virtuous because the year is in accordance with the virtue of Jia and Yin. This is also due to their innate qualities and the influence of their parents' energies. In ancient times, the primordial energy was pure, so there were many sages. In modern times, the customs are corrupt and the atmosphere is turbid, so there are no more sages.]

    In Yuzhang Prefecture, among the people of high social standing, there were several women who exposed their faces on the road, struggling to give even the smallest amount to their husbands, providing carriages, horses, clothes, and provisions, and even being elected for filial piety and integrity. However, instead of rewarding the virtuous, they chose the rich, giving everything to those who came later, despite the years of hard work and the presence of wives and children filling the house. They were dismissed and replaced by later people.

    In the remote and secluded areas of distant mountainous regions, honey and wax are produced. People often use buckets to collect bees, doing so annually. In these regions, they make wooden containers with small holes, coating the inside and outside with honey and wax. During the spring when bees are about to reproduce, they capture a few and place them in the containers. As the bees fly away, they bring more companions. Over time, the number of bees increases, and eventually, they carry the containers back home.

    Those who sleep while wearing a belt dream of snakes. [51]

    鸟衔人之发梦飞
    　　王尒张衡马均皆冒重雾行一人无恙一人病一人死问其故无恙人曰我饮酒病者食死者空腹
    　　人以冷水自渍至膝可顿啖数十枚瓜渍至腰啖转多至颈可啖百余枚所渍水皆作瓜气味此事未试人中酒不解治之以汤自渍即愈汤亦作酒气味也
    　　昔刘石于中山酒家沽酒酒家与千日酒忘言其节度归至家当醉而家人不知以为死也权葬之酒家计千日满乃忆石前来酤酒醉当醒耳徃视之云石亡来三年已葬于是开棺醉始醒俗云石饮酒一醉千日
    　　旧説云天河与海通近世有人居海濵者年年八月有浮槎去来不失期人有竒志立飞阁于槎上多赍粮乘槎而去十余日中犹观星月日辰自后芒芒忽忽亦不觉昼夜去十余日奄至一处有城郭状屋舍甚严遥望宫中多织妇见一丈夫牵牛渚次饮之牵牛人乃惊问曰何由至此此人具説来意并问此是何处答曰君还至蜀郡访严君平则知之竟不上岸因还如期后至蜀问君平曰某年月日有客星犯牵牛宿计年月正是此人到天河时也 [52]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [52]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 鳥衔人之发梦飞 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'birds carrying human hair flying in dreams,' possibly symbolizing the transmission of dreams or messages through birds.
    • 王尔张衡马均皆冒重雾行一人无恙一人病一人死问其故无恙人曰我饮酒病者食死者空腹 - Complex and elliptical sentence describing three individuals (王尔, 张衡, 马均) encountering heavy fog and experiencing different outcomes (one unharmed, one ill, one dead). The explanation for the outcomes is that the unharmed person drank, the ill person ate, and the dead person was fasting.
    • 人以冷水自渍至膝可顿啖数十枚瓜渍至腰啖转多至颈可啖百余枚所渍水皆作瓜气味此事未试人中酒不解治之以汤自渍即愈汤亦作酒气味也 - Describes a practice of soaking oneself in cold water to eat more melons, with the water turning into the smell of melons. Also mentions that if someone is drunk, soaking in hot water can cure them, with the water taking on the smell of alcohol.
    • 昔刘玄石于中山酒家沽酒酒家与千日酒忘言其节度归至家当醉而家人不知以为死也权葬之酒家计千日满乃忆玄石前来酤酒醉当醒耳往视之云玄石亡来三年已葬于是开棺醉始醒俗云玄石饮酒一醉千日 - Legends of Liu Xuanzhi, who drank a special wine that put him to sleep for a thousand days. The wine seller forgot to mention this, and when Liu returned home, his family thought he was dead and buried him. After a thousand days, the wine seller remembered and went to check, finding Liu still asleep in the coffin. Upon opening it, Liu woke up, and the story became famous.
    • 旧说云天河与海通近世有人居海滨者年年八月有浮槎去来不失期人有奇志立飞阁于槎上多赍粮乘槎而去十余日中犹观星月日辰自后茫茫忽忽亦不觉昼夜去十余日奄至一处有城郭状屋舍甚严遥望宫中多织妇见一丈夫牵牛渚次饮之牵牛人乃惊问何由至此此人具说来意并问此是何处答曰君还至蜀郡访严君平则知之竟不上岸因还如期后至蜀问君平曰某年月日有客星犯牵牛宿计年月正是此人到天河时也 - Describes a traveler who boards a floating raft (浮槎) to explore the heavens. After traveling for over ten days, he arrives at a celestial city where he encounters a man who is milking a cow. The traveler asks how he got there, and the man directs him to consult a sage named Yan Junping in Shu Commandery. The traveler returns and later confirms that the journey coincided with a celestial event.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 王尔, 张衡, 马均 - Historical figures, with 张衡 being a famous astronomer and inventor from the Han dynasty. The other two figures are less well-known but are likely historical or legendary figures.
    • 刘玄石 - A legendary figure associated with the story of the 'thousand-day wine,' which is a famous Chinese legend.
    • 天河与海通 - Refers to the mythological connection between the Milky Way (天河) and the sea, a common theme in Chinese folklore.
    • 严君平 - A famous sage and astrologer from the Han dynasty, known for his wisdom and ability to predict the future.
    • 牵牛 - Refers to the celestial being associated with the Milky Way, often depicted as a cowherd in Chinese mythology.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The sentence '王尔张衡马均皆冒重雾行一人无恙一人病一人死问其故无恙人曰我饮酒病者食死者空腹' is elliptical and requires careful parsing to understand the sequence of events and the reasoning behind the outcomes.
    • The sentence '人以冷水自渍至膝可顿啖数十枚瓜渍至腰啖转多至颈可啖百余枚所渍水皆作瓜气味此事未试人中酒不解治之以汤自渍即愈汤亦作酒气味也' is complex and describes a practice that requires careful explanation to make sense in English.
    • The story '昔刘玄石于中山酒家沽酒酒家与千日酒忘言其节度归至家当醉而家人不知以为死也权葬之酒家计千日满乃忆玄石前来酤酒醉当醒耳往视之云玄石亡来三年已葬于是开棺醉始醒俗云玄石饮酒一醉千日' is narrated in a compact and elliptical style, requiring careful unpacking to convey the full narrative.
    • The passage '旧说云天河与海通近世有人居海滨者年年八月有浮槎去来不失期人有奇志立飞阁于槎上多赍粮乘槎而去十余日中犹观星月日辰自后茫茫忽忽亦不觉昼夜去十余日奄至一处有城郭状屋舍甚严遥望宫中多织妇见一丈夫牵牛渚次饮之牵牛人乃惊问何由至此此人具说来意并问此是何处答曰君还至蜀郡访严君平则知之竟不上岸因还如期后至蜀问君平曰某年月日有客星犯牵牛宿计年月正是此人到天河时也' is highly metaphorical and requires careful interpretation to convey the traveler's journey and the celestial encounter.

    Proper Nouns

    • 王尔, 张衡, 马均 - Historical or legendary figures.
    • 刘玄石 - A legendary figure associated with the 'thousand-day wine' story.
    • 严君平 - A famous sage and astrologer from the Han dynasty.
    • 牵牛 - A celestial being associated with the Milky Way.

    Birds Carrying Human Hair in Dreams

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, Wang Er, Zhang Heng, and Ma Jun all encountered heavy fog while traveling. One remained unharmed, one fell ill, and one died. When asked why, the unharmed one said he had drunk, the ill one had eaten, and the dead one was fasting.

    A person soaked themselves in cold water up to their knees and could eat dozens of melons in one go. Soaking up to the waist allowed them to eat even more, up to the neck, enabling them to eat over a hundred melons. The water they soaked in turned into the smell of melons. This has not been tested, but if someone is drunk, soaking in hot water can cure them, and the water will also take on the smell of alcohol.

    In ancient times, Liu Xuanzhi, a man from Zhongshan, bought wine at a tavern. The tavern owner gave him a special wine that put him to sleep for a thousand days but forgot to mention this. Liu returned home, and his family thought he was dead and buried him. After a thousand days, the tavern owner remembered and went to check, finding Liu still asleep in the coffin. Upon opening it, Liu woke up, and the story became famous. It is said that Liu Xuanzhi drank a wine that put him to sleep for a thousand days.

    An old saying goes that the Milky Way connects with the sea. In recent times, there was a man who lived by the seaside. Every August, he would board a floating raft that traveled to and from the heavens without fail. With a bold ambition, he built a flying pavilion on the raft, carried provisions, and set off. For over ten days, he still observed the stars, moon, and sun. After that, he felt a sense of detachment and did not notice the passage of day and night. After more than ten days, he suddenly arrived at a place with a city-like appearance and well-ordered houses. From afar, he saw many women weaving in the palace and a man milking a cow. The man was surprised and asked how he had come there. The traveler explained his intentions and asked where he was. The man replied, "When you return, go to Shu Commandery and consult Yan Junping to find out." The traveler did not disembark and returned on schedule. Later, he went to Shu and asked Yan Junping, "On a certain day, a guest star invaded the position of the Cowherd. Calculating the time, it was the same day as when this man arrived in the heavens." [52]

    人有山行堕深涧者无出路饥饿欲死左右见蛇甚多朝暮引颈向东方人因伏地学之遂不饥体殊轻便能登岩岸经数年后竦身举臂遂超出涧上即得还家顔色悦怿颇更黠慧胜故还食谷啖滋味百余日中复本质
    　　天门郡有幽山峻谷而其土人有从下经过者忽然踊出林表状如飞仙遂絶迹谷中如此甚数遂名此处为仙谷有乐道好事者入此谷中洗沐以求飞仙徃徃得去有长生意思人疑恐是妖怪乃以大石自坠牵一犬入谷中犬复飞去其人还告乡里募数十人执杖揭山草伐木至山顶观之遥见一物长数十丈其高隐人耳如簸箕格射刺杀之所吞人骨积此左右已成封其蟒开口广丈余前后失人皆此蟒气所噏上于是此地遂安稳无患

    　　博物志卷十 [53]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [53]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 人有山行堕深涧者 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'a person who falls into a deep ravine while traveling through the mountains.'
    • 无出路饥饿欲死 - Means 'with no way out, starving to death,' describing the dire situation of the person.
    • 左右见龟蛇甚多 - Means 'seeing many turtles and snakes on both sides,' indicating the environment around the person.
    • 朝暮引颈向东方 - Means 'stretching their necks toward the east every morning and evening,' describing the behavior of the turtles and snakes.
    • 人因伏地学之 - Means 'the person then crouched down and imitated them,' indicating the person's action in response to the turtles and snakes.
    • 遂不饥体殊轻便 - Means 'and thus did not feel hungry, and his body became unusually light and agile,' describing the effect of the person's actions.
    • 能登岩岸 - Means 'he could climb rocky shores,' indicating the person's newfound ability.
    • 经数年后竦身举臂遂超出涧上 - Means 'after several years, he leaped up and raised his arms, and thus leaped out of the ravine,' describing the person's final escape.
    • 即得还家顔色悦怿颇更黠慧胜故 - Means 'he then returned home, with a happy and joyful expression, much more clever and wise than before,' describing the person's transformation.
    • 胜故还食谷啖滋味百余日中复本质 - Means 'he returned to eating grains and savoring flavors, and after over a hundred days, he resumed his original nature,' describing the person's return to normal life.
    • 天门郡有幽山峻谷 - Means 'in Tianmen County, there is a secluded mountain and steep ravine,' setting the scene for the second narrative.
    • 而其土人有从下经过者 - Means 'and there are local people who pass through from below,' introducing the second group of characters.
    • 忽然踊出林表状如飞仙 - Means 'suddenly they leap out of the forest, appearing like flying immortals,' describing the mysterious appearance of the people.
    • 遂绝迹谷中如此甚数 - Means 'thus they disappear from the ravine, and this happens many times,' indicating the frequency of the phenomenon.
    • 遂名此处为仙谷 - Means 'thus this place is named 'Immortal Ravine,' describing the naming of the location.
    • 有乐道好事者入此谷中洗沐以求飞仙 - Means 'there are those who love the Dao and seek good fortune who enter this ravine to bathe in hopes of becoming immortals,' describing the actions of the seekers.
    • 往往得去 - Means 'they often succeed,' indicating the success of the seekers.
    • 有长生意思人疑恐是妖怪 - Means 'those with the intention of living forever suspect it might be a demon,' describing the skepticism of some people.
    • 乃以大石自坠牵一犬入谷中 - Means 'so they throw a large stone to fall and drag a dog into the ravine,' describing the experiment conducted to test the phenomenon.
    • 犬复飞去 - Means 'the dog also flies away,' describing the result of the experiment.
    • 其人还告乡里募数十人执杖揭山草伐木至山顶观之 - Means 'the person returns and tells the village, gathering dozens of people with sticks, clearing mountain grass and cutting trees to the top of the mountain to observe,' describing the effort to investigate the phenomenon.
    • 遥见一物长数十丈其高隐人耳如簸箕格射刺杀之所吞人骨积此左右 - Means 'from afar, they see an object tens of zhang long, so high it is hidden from sight, like a winnowing basket, where arrows and spears are shot and killed, with human bones piled up on both sides,' describing the appearance of the creature.
    • 已成封其蟒开口广丈余前后失人皆此蟒气所噏上 - Means 'it has already been sealed as a python, with its mouth wide open over ten zhang, and those who go in and out are all sucked up by the python's breath,' describing the final state of the creature.
    • 于是此地遂安稳无患 - Means 'thus this place becomes peaceful and free of harm,' describing the outcome of the investigation.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 龟蛇 - Turtles and snakes are often associated with longevity and immortality in Chinese culture.
    • 飞仙 - Flying immortals are mythical beings in Chinese folklore, often associated with Daoist practices.
    • 长生意思 - The pursuit of immortality is a common theme in Chinese Daoist and alchemical traditions.
    • 妖怪 - Demons or supernatural beings that are often feared and avoided in Chinese folklore.
    • 天门郡 - A historical place name in China, though specific details about its location and significance are not provided in the text.

    Proper Nouns

    • 天门郡 - A historical place name in China.
    • 仙谷 - The name given to the ravine due to the mysterious phenomenon.

    A person traveling through the mountains fell into a deep ravine, with no way out and starving to death. On both sides, he saw many turtles and snakes, stretching their necks toward the east every morning and evening. The person then crouched down and imitated them, and thus did not feel hungry, and his body became unusually light and agile, allowing him to climb rocky shores. After several years, he leaped up and raised his arms, and thus leaped out of the ravine, returning home with a happy and joyful expression, much more clever and wise than before. He returned to eating grains and savoring flavors, and after over a hundred days, he resumed his original nature.

    In Tianmen County, there is a secluded mountain and steep ravine. There are local people who pass through from below, and suddenly they leap out of the forest, appearing like flying immortals, thus disappearing from the ravine, and this happens many times. Thus, this place is named "Immortal Ravine." There are those who love the Dao and seek good fortune who enter this ravine to bathe in hopes of becoming immortals, and they often succeed. Those with the intention of living forever suspect it might be a demon, so they throw a large stone to fall and drag a dog into the ravine. The dog also flies away. The person returns and tells the village, gathering dozens of people with sticks, clearing mountain grass and cutting trees to the top of the mountain to observe. From afar, they see an object tens of zhang long, so high it is hidden from sight, like a winnowing basket, where arrows and spears are shot and killed, with human bones piled up on both sides. It has already been sealed as a python, with its mouth wide open over ten zhang, and those who go in and out are all sucked up by the python's breath. Thus, this place becomes peaceful and free of harm.

    [53]

    人有山行堕深涧者无出路饥饿欲死左右见蛇甚多朝暮引颈向东方人因伏地学之遂不饥体殊轻便能登岩岸经数年后竦身举臂遂超出涧上即得还家顔色悦怿颇更黠慧胜故还食谷啖滋味百余日中复本质
    　　天门郡有幽山峻谷而其土人有从下经过者忽然踊出林表状如飞仙遂絶迹谷中如此甚数遂名此处为仙谷有乐道好事者入此谷中洗沐以求飞仙徃徃得去有长生意思人疑恐是妖怪乃以大石自坠牵一犬入谷中犬复飞去其人还告乡里募数十人执杖揭山草伐木至山顶观之遥见一物长数十丈其高隐人耳如簸箕格射刺杀之所吞人骨积此左右已成封其蟒开口广丈余前后失人皆此蟒气所噏上于是此地遂安稳无患

    　　博物志卷十

    A person traveling through the mountains fell into a deep ravine, with no way out and starving to death. On both sides, he saw many turtles and snakes, stretching their necks toward the east every morning and evening. The person then crouched down and imitated them, and thus did not feel hungry, and his body became unusually light and agile, allowing him to climb rocky shores. After several years, he leaped up and raised his arms, and thus leaped out of the ravine, returning home with a happy and joyful expression, much more clever and wise than before. He returned to eating grains and savoring flavors, and after over a hundred days, he resumed his original nature.

    In Tianmen County, there is a secluded mountain and steep ravine. There are local people who pass through from below, and suddenly they leap out of the forest, appearing like flying immortals, thus disappearing from the ravine, and this happens many times. Thus, this place is named "Immortal Ravine." There are those who love the Dao and seek good fortune who enter this ravine to bathe in hopes of becoming immortals, and they often succeed. Those with the intention of living forever suspect it might be a demon, so they throw a large stone to fall and drag a dog into the ravine. The dog also flies away. The person returns and tells the village, gathering dozens of people with sticks, clearing mountain grass and cutting trees to the top of the mountain to observe. From afar, they see an object tens of zhang long, so high it is hidden from sight, like a winnowing basket, where arrows and spears are shot and killed, with human bones piled up on both sides. It has already been sealed as a python, with its mouth wide open over ten zhang, and those who go in and out are all sucked up by the python's breath. Thus, this place becomes peaceful and free of harm.
